prompt: Create a vintage Art Deco travel poster for Mumbai, India. Use a vertical 2:3 poster canvas with a thin double-line border and aged paper texture in warm ochre, sepia, and sunset gold. At the very top, place huge bold condensed serif lettering reading “MUMBAI” in dark teal, spanning almost the full width. Directly below it on the left, add widely spaced uppercase red letters reading “INDIA”. Beneath that, include a simple line-art rising sun icon, then an italic tagline reading “The City That Never Sleeps.” and a small decorative divider with one centered dot. Under the divider, add coordinates text reading 19.0760° N, 72.8777° E. The lower two-thirds should show a cinematic sunset view of the Gateway of India on the waterfront, rendered in realistic vintage-poster style: the monument is large on the right-center with its arches and turrets visible, glowing orange sky and clouds behind it, sun low near the horizon, shimmering water with small boats, and a curving seaside road along Marine Drive in the foreground. Include exactly one black-and-yellow vintage taxi on the road in the lower foreground, small city skyline buildings on the left background, palm trees, street lamps, a stone seawall, and scattered tiny pedestrians near the monument. Keep the typography clean and legible, with nostalgic grain, slightly faded ink, subtle halftone texture, and no modern objects, logos, or extra text.
